title: Tenant Rights Information/Counseling * Domestic Violence Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Tenant Rights Information/Counseling * Domestic Violence Issues 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Domestic Violence Issues Mobile Home Residents No Filter Domestic Violence Issues Mobile Home Residents Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and representation on domestic abuse and family law matters. Information available in regard to immigration, public benefits, tenant/landlord disputes, discrimination law, and contested restraining orders. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON HOME FREE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual intervention program that includes services such as: -Emergency Services: Hotline, information and referral, and telephone advocacy -Housing Services: Home visits, long-term advocacy and support, help to locate and maintain safe housing -Domestic and sexual violence healthcare and advocacy -Court and legal advocacy with a restraining order hotline -Economic Empowerment: Credit building and repair advocacy, savings, interview skills, employment seekers group, barrier reduction assistance, and Rent Well landlord-tenant education Motel vouchers are only available for those in emergencies who are actively fleeing domestic violence. Availability is dependent upon funding. Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Emergency Hotline: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Restraining Order Services: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 235-8655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE
